rules.description: Base image uses ':latest' tag or has no tag
rules.recommendation: Pin your base image to a specific version (e.g. python:3.11-slim) to guarantee reproducibility.

rules.description: Container runs as root user (no USER instruction or USER root)
rules.recommendation: Add a non-root USER instruction before CMD/ENTRYPOINT (e.g. RUN useradd -m appuser && USER appuser).

rules.description: Multiple consecutive RUN instructions create unnecessary layers
rules.recommendation: Chain RUN commands with '&&' and '\' to reduce the number of image layers.

rules.description: ADD instruction used instead of COPY
rules.recommendation: Replace ADD with COPY unless you specifically need ADD's tar-extraction or URL-fetching behaviour.

rules.description: No WORKDIR instruction defined
rules.recommendation: Define a WORKDIR to set a clear working directory and avoid ambiguity in file paths.

rules.description: apt-get update and apt-get install in separate RUN instructions
rules.recommendation: Combine 'apt-get update' and 'apt-get install' in a single RUN instruction to avoid stale package lists.

rules.description: No HEALTHCHECK instruction defined, preventing Docker from monitoring container health
rules.recommendation: Add a HEALTHCHECK instruction to allow Docker to monitor the container's runtime state and restart it if unhealthy. Source: IBM Best Practices for Docker Security.

rules.description: apt-get install used without --no-install-recommends flag, pulling unnecessary packages
rules.recommendation: Add '--no-install-recommends' to 'apt-get install' to minimise image size by only installing explicitly required packages. Source: 10 Docker Best Practices (0xfujin).

rules.description: pip install used without --no-cache-dir flag, leaving pip cache in the image layer
rules.recommendation: Add '--no-cache-dir' to 'pip install' to avoid storing pip's download cache in the image, reducing final image size. Source: 10 Docker Best Practices (0xfujin).

rules.description: Image has no labels (maintainer, version, etc.)
rules.recommendation: Add LABEL instructions for at least 'maintainer' and 'version' to improve traceability.

rules.description: Image contains sensitive data in environment variables (password, secret, key)
rules.recommendation: Never store secrets in ENV instructions. Use Docker secrets, a .env file, or a secrets manager instead.

rules.description: Image has an excessive number of layers (> 20)
rules.recommendation: Reduce the number of layers by combining RUN instructions and using multi-stage builds.

rules.description: Image runs as root user
rules.recommendation: Ensure the image's USER is set to a non-root account in the Dockerfile.

rules.description: Base image uses ':latest' tag or has no tag
rules.recommendation: Pin the base image to a specific digest or version tag.

rules.description: Service uses an image with ':latest' tag or no tag
rules.recommendation: Pin service images to a specific version tag to ensure reproducible deployments.

rules.description: Service runs as root or has no 'user' defined
rules.recommendation: Define a non-root 'user' for each service (e.g. user: '1000').

rules.description: Service exposes a plaintext password or secret in environment variables
rules.recommendation: Use Docker secrets or a .env file instead of hardcoding credentials in docker-compose.yml.

rules.description: Service exposes a port on all interfaces (0.0.0.0) or a sensitive port
rules.recommendation: Bind ports to a specific interface (e.g. 127.0.0.1:8080:80) and avoid exposing privileged ports externally.

rules.description: Duplicate service images detected (possible redundancy)
rules.recommendation: Review whether duplicate service images are intentional; consider merging redundant services.

rules.description: Service runs in privileged mode, granting full root capabilities on the host
rules.recommendation: Remove 'privileged: true' and use fine-grained 'cap_add' for only the capabilities the container requires. Source: IBM Best Practices for Docker Security.

rules.description: Service mounts a volume without read-only flag for non-data directories
rules.recommendation: Mount volumes as read-only (:ro) wherever write access is not needed to reduce the container's attack surface. Source: IBM Best Practices for Docker Security.

rules.description: Service has no explicit replica count defined in deploy configuration
rules.recommendation: Define an explicit 'replicas' value under 'deploy' to control service scaling and avoid ambiguity.

rules.description: Service has no CPU or memory limits defined (resources.limits)
rules.recommendation: Define 'resources.limits' with CPU and memory caps to prevent a single service from monopolising cluster resources.

rules.description: Service has no CPU or memory reservations defined (resources.reservations)
rules.recommendation: Define 'resources.reservations' to guarantee a minimum allocation and improve scheduling predictability.

rules.description: Service has no restart policy defined or no bounded max_attempts
rules.recommendation: Define a 'restart_policy' with 'condition', 'delay', and bounded 'max_attempts' to avoid infinite restart loops destabilising nodes.

rules.description: Service has no placement constraints defined
rules.recommendation: Use 'placement.constraints' to control service scheduling across nodes (e.g., pin database services to specific nodes).

rules.description: Service has no rolling update configuration (update_config)
rules.recommendation: Define 'update_config' with 'parallelism' and 'delay' to ensure zero-downtime deployments during rolling updates.

rules.description: Service passes sensitive data (passwords, secrets, API keys) via environment variables instead of Docker Swarm secrets
rules.recommendation: Use the Docker Swarm secrets mechanism instead of environment variables for sensitive data. Env vars may be visible in logs or 'docker inspect' output.

rules.description: Service uses ':latest' tag or unversioned image, undermining deployment reproducibility
rules.recommendation: Pin service images to a specific version tag or digest to guarantee reproducible deployments across all Swarm nodes.

rules.description: Service relies on the default network instead of an explicitly defined overlay network
rules.recommendation: Attach services to explicitly defined overlay networks to enforce proper service isolation and avoid unintended cross-service communication.

rules.description: Service has no health check defined, preventing Swarm from detecting and replacing unhealthy tasks
rules.recommendation: Define a 'healthcheck' with 'test', 'interval', 'timeout', and 'retries' to allow Swarm's scheduler to detect and replace unhealthy tasks.

rules.description: Service has no logging driver configuration defined, making log aggregation and monitoring difficult
rules.recommendation: Define a 'logging' section with an appropriate driver (json-file, syslog, fluentd) and 'max-size'/'max-file' options to enable structured log collection and prevent runaway disk usage. Source: AccuWeb Swarm Best Practices, IBM Docker Security.

rules.description: Service runs in privileged mode, granting unrestricted access to the host system
rules.recommendation: Remove 'privileged: true' and use the minimum required capabilities via 'cap_add'. Privileged containers bypass all security isolation. Source: IBM Best Practices for Docker Security.

rules.description: apt-get install used without footprint cleanup
rules.recommendation: Add 'rm -rf /var/lib/apt/lists/*' after 'apt-get install' to reduce image tracking bloat.

rules.description: apk add used without --no-cache
rules.recommendation: Use 'apk add --no-cache' instead of maintaining apk caches.

rules.description: yum install used without repository cleanup
rules.recommendation: Add 'yum clean all' after 'yum install' to purge fetched package caches.

rules.description: curl used without silent-fail flags (-fSL)
rules.recommendation: Use 'curl -fSL' to ensure Docker builds fail fast if the remote asset returns HTTP errors.

rules.description: wget used without quiet-output stream bindings
rules.recommendation: Use 'wget -qO-' to streamline binary streaming securely in build contexts.

rules.description: sudo executed during Docker build
rules.recommendation: Do not use 'sudo' in Dockerfiles. Explicitly switch users via the 'USER' command.

rules.description: No EXPOSE network port mappings declared
rules.recommendation: Explicitly define 'EXPOSE <port>' to improve port discovery and document container intent.

rules.description: Use of deprecated MAINTAINER tag
rules.recommendation: Replace legacy 'MAINTAINER' directives with standardized 'LABEL author=' format.

rules.description: Inline path traversals (cd) rather than deterministic WORKDIR
rules.recommendation: Use explicit 'WORKDIR /path' statements instead of calling 'cd' to maintain execution determinism.

rules.description: npm install used without clearing node caching
rules.recommendation: Execute 'npm cache clean --force' after 'npm install' to destroy temporary JavaScript dependency layers.

rules.description: Service missing declarative restart parameter
rules.recommendation: Define a static 'restart' recovery policy (e.g., 'unless-stopped').

rules.description: Service missing a localized healthcheck configuration
rules.recommendation: Define healthchecks directly in Compose or enforce them at the Image level.

rules.description: Service assigns container to host network mode
rules.recommendation: Avoid 'network_mode: host'. Use mapped ports to preserve software-defined-network isolation.

rules.description: Service breaks PID namespace via host mapping
rules.recommendation: Remove 'pid: host'. Sharing the host process ID space allows container root escalation easily.

rules.description: Service ignores structured resource memory/CPU limitations
rules.recommendation: Dictate execution footprints through 'deploy.resources.limits' to avoid node locking failures.

rules.description: Explicit container_name configuration injected
rules.recommendation: Remove 'container_name'. Forced nomenclature prevents horizontal scaling and replicas generation.

rules.description: SYS_ADMIN or ALL capability escalation granted
rules.recommendation: Do not grant root-equivalent system capabilities such as 'SYS_ADMIN' globally via 'cap_add'.

rules.description: Inter-Process Communication attached directly to Host
rules.recommendation: Avoid 'ipc: host' to ensure memory segment namespaces remain sealed against the hosting system.

rules.description: Fixed deployment bounded by hardcoded MAC identifiers
rules.recommendation: Avoid pinning strict 'mac_address' configurations as it impedes scheduling fluidity globally.

rules.description: DNS queries strictly overridden skipping internal resolvers
rules.recommendation: Refrain from hardcoding public DNS references (like 8.8.8.8) to maintain resolution independence.

rules.description: Service stop_grace_period configuration is absent
rules.recommendation: Implement 'stop_grace_period' dictations to perform safe termination signaling in clustered tears.

rules.description: Swarm routing mesh bypassed using internal dnsrr mode
rules.recommendation: Use 'vip' (Virtual IP) mode instead of 'dnsrr' to retain layer-4 load-balancing topologies.

rules.description: Update ordering follows default stop-first pipeline causing down-time
rules.recommendation: Configure 'update_config.order' as 'start-first' for automated Zero-Downtime rollouts.

rules.description: Restart temporal delays unbounded or suspiciously brief
rules.recommendation: Include a minimum temporal 'delay' variable representing execution recovery to avoid failure loop spinbacks.

rules.description: Swarm deployment volume omits constraint isolation typing
rules.recommendation: Use explicit volume structures ('type: volume|bind') when deploying storage to clusters.

rules.description: Total image payload significantly exceeds 1GB limit
rules.recommendation: Use multi-stage builds alongside minimal architectural footprints targeting sub-500MB sizing constructs.

rules.description: Virtual network mappings lack ExposedPorts structure definitions
rules.recommendation: Images intending to route traffic must embed explicit EXPOSE boundaries in their configuration schemas.

rules.description: Scratch infrastructure defaults silently back to unregulated ROOT mappings
rules.recommendation: Even scratch deployments executing native go-binaries require constrained isolated UID mapping identities injected post-build.

rules.description: Production configurations rely implicitly upon 'latest' tag allocations
rules.recommendation: Immutable images must omit the 'latest' string mapping internally to preserve determinism validation logic.

rules.description: Constructed object artifacts miss active daemon process triggers (CMD/ENTRYPOINT)
rules.recommendation: Ensure an explicit execution directive dictates parameter parsing behaviors using static ENTRYPOINT formats.
